title: Axios Supply Chain — RAT via plain-crypto-js
description: Maintainer account compromised (Sapphire Sleet / North Korea). These versions inject plain-crypto-js@4.2.1 which runs a postinstall script that deploys a cross-platform Remote Access Trojan (RAT) on macOS, Windows, and Linux. Malware phones home to sfrclak.com:8000 and self-deletes to evade forensics. Rotate ALL credentials if installed.

title: plain-crypto-js — Malicious RAT Dropper
description: Not a legitimate package. Created by Sapphire Sleet as a delivery vehicle for the Axios supply chain attack. Contains postinstall hook that deploys WAVESHAPER.V2 backdoor. Never a dependency of real Axios. If found in node_modules, assume the host is fully compromised.

title: @shadanai/openclaw — Vendors plain-crypto-js payload
description: Found to vendor the malicious plain-crypto-js payload directly. Part of the same Axios supply chain attack campaign.

title: @qqbrowser/openclaw-qbot — Ships tampered axios@1.14.1
description: Bundles a tampered axios@1.14.1 with plain-crypto-js injected as a dependency in its node_modules folder. Same RAT campaign.

title: Shai-Hulud — Self-replicating npm worm (Sept 2025)
description: Patient-zero of the Shai-Hulud worm. Steals GitHub/npm/cloud credentials at install time, then uses victim's npm token to publish malicious versions of OTHER packages they own — propagating automatically. ~180+ packages compromised, ~$50M in crypto stolen. Rotate ALL credentials on any host that installed this version.

title: Shai-Hulud worm — Compromised propagation target
description: Compromised by the Shai-Hulud worm via stolen maintainer credentials. Treat any installed version as fully compromised.

title: Shai-Hulud 2.0 — preinstall payload (Nov 2025)
description: Compromised in the November 2025 Shai-Hulud 2.0 wave that hit Zapier, ENS, AsyncAPI, PostHog, Browserbase and Postman packages. Payload moved from postinstall to preinstall to bypass scanners. Created 25k+ malicious GitHub repos across ~350 victim accounts.

title: Lazarus / DPRK — Marstech Mayhem credential stealer
description: Published by fake recruiters on LinkedIn targeting developers. Steals Discord tokens, browser cookies/passwords, and crypto wallet seed phrases on Win/macOS/Linux. ~10k downloads before discovery. Part of an ongoing North Korean campaign.

title: event-stream — Crypto wallet theft (2018)
description: Malicious maintainer added flatmap-stream dependency targeting Copay Bitcoin wallets. Historic supply chain attack.

title: node-ipc — Protestware targeting Russian/Belarusian IPs (2022)
description: Maintainer intentionally introduced code that wiped files on systems with Russian or Belarusian IP addresses.
